citations.narrative:
The licensee did not notify USDA Animal Care prior to moving their animals to a new address. Upon arriving at the
address listed for site 002, the APHIS official found the building empty. When the APHIS official called the licensee, they
stated that they had relocated at the beginning of the year.
Failure to notify Animal Care of a change in location can make it difficult to verify regulated activities and ensure the
health and wellbeing of regulated animals. Licenses are issued to specific persons, and are issued for specific activities,
types and numbers of animals, and approved sites. A licensee shall notify Animal Care no fewer than 90 days and obtain
a new license before any change in the name, address, substantial control or ownership of his or her business or
operation, locations, activities, and number or type of animals described in paragraph (b)(2) of this section.
To be corrected from this day, May 8, 2026, forward.

citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
A responsible adult was not available to accompany APHIS Officials during the inspection process at 1:10 PM on
5/6/2026. The inspector attempted to contact the licensee by knocking on the facility door. While at the door, the inspector
saw that the building was empty. The inspector called the licensee and they said that they had moved and were no longer
at the address. Failure to provide access to the facility, animals, and records for inspection is a violation of the AWA and
regulations. Continued failure to provide access is serious and may result in enforcement action.
End Section

narrative:
2.1(b)(1) 
   
Requirements and application 
The licensee did not notify USDA Animal Care prior to moving their animals to a new address. Upon arriving at the 
address listed for site 002, the APHIS official found the building empty. When the APHIS official called the licensee, they 
stated that they had relocated at the beginning of the year. 
 
Failure to notify Animal Care of a change in location can make it difficult to verify regulated activities and ensure the 
health and wellbeing of regulated animals. Licenses are issued to specific persons, and are issued for specific activities, 
types and numbers of animals, and approved sites. A licensee shall notify Animal Care no fewer than 90 days and obtain 
a new license before any change in the name, address, substantial control or ownership of his or her business or 
operation, locations, activities, and number or type of animals described in paragraph (b)(2) of this section. 
To be corrected from this day, May 8, 2026, forward. 
2.126(b) 
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
A responsible adult was not available to accompany APHIS Officials during the inspection process at 1:10 PM on 
5/6/2026. The inspector attempted to contact the licensee by knocking on the facility door. While at the door, the inspector 
saw that the building was empty. The inspector called the licensee and they said that they had moved and were no longer 
at the address. Failure to provide access to the facility, animals, and records for inspection is a violation of the AWA and 
regulations. Continued failure to provide access is serious and may result in enforcement action. 
 
 
End Section
